# easyChef Pro — Product Marketing Context
# SOURCE: Sheet EC-2026-001 · Read 2026-05-15 · Do not edit manually

## Product
**Name:** easyChef Pro
**Category:** Meal planning + pantry management app (iOS, Android)
**Company:** Digital Galactica Labs
**Launch date:** July 1, 2026
**Status:** Pre-launch waitlist — founding family program open

## Tagline (locked)
Your kitchen. In command.

## The Loop (locked brand architecture)
TRACK → PLAN → OPTIMIZE → COOK → SHOP
"The loop closes. Every week."

## Active LPs
- LP-A `/lp/waitlist-a` — ICP: super_mom_money · angle: savings · headline: "Stop the Invisible Grocery Leak."
- LP-B `/lp/waitlist-b` — ICP: super_mom_time · angle: time_relief · headline: "Dinner Decided Before You Open the Fridge."

## ICP
- **super_mom_money:** Spends $300+/month on groceries, sees $111/month disappear to waste. Wants the leak closed.
- **super_mom_time:** Primary household decision-maker, 6:30 PM dinner scramble every night. Wants the decision made before they walk in.

## Approved Claims (ACTIVE, approved by Taylor)

### Savings
- "The average family saves $1,336 a year. That is $111 back every single month."
- "$111 a month back in your pocket. Every month. Just from not throwing food away."
- "Eight dollars a month. A hundred and eleven dollars back. That is not a subscription. That is a return."
- "$111 in groceries thrown away every month. Not because you forgot to shop. Because there was no system."

### Waste
- "The food in your fridge gets used. 69.5% less of it ends up in the trash."
- "The spinach at the back of your fridge is three days from going bad. easyChef Pro already planned dinner around it."
- "Nothing hides at the back of the fridge anymore."
- "Dinner starts with what you already have. The shopping list fills in the gaps."

### Speed
- "Fridge to table. Thirty minutes."
- "The fridge opens. Dinner is already decided. That is a different feeling."
- "No more standing at an open fridge at 6:30 PM with nothing decided."
- "One Sunday hour. Seven nights sorted."

### Product
- "800,000 products. Verified. Ready when you are."
- "Scan the receipt. Your pantry is already updated."
- "No daily logging. No tracking what you ate. Your kitchen just knows."
- "The meal plan starts with what you already have. The shopping list fills in the gaps."
- "One tap checkout at Walmart. More stores coming."
- "A full week of dinners. Built from what is already in your kitchen."

### Credibility
- "Tested across 10,000 real households before a single family paid for it."
- "Three years. Nine patent-pending technologies filed with the USPTO. We got it right before we shipped it."
- "Every food in our database was verified by a registered dietitian. Not crowdsourced. Not guessed."
- "Powered by Digital Nutrition Intelligence™ — clinical grade nutrition data, the same standard used in medical and dietitian practice."
- "Built by first responders."

### Pricing (approved exact wording)
- "$7.99 a month. Locked forever." — founding price
- "$19.99 a month after founding." — standard price
- "60% off the standard price." — founding discount
- "The first 5,000 families lock in at $7.99 forever. After that the price goes to $19.99."

### Positioning
- "Five apps open on your phone. One does what all five should."
- "A meal planner tells you what to cook. easyChef Pro runs your kitchen."
- "Every other food app stops at one step. This one closes the whole loop."
- "No ads. Not now. Not ever."

### Outcomes
- "Walk into the evening already knowing what is for dinner."
- "Running a kitchen should not feel like a second job."
- "You already made a hundred decisions today. Dinner should not be another one."

### Trial
- "Seven days. Full access. No credit card."
- "The trial runs the full loop. TRACK. PLAN. OPTIMIZE. COOK. SHOP. Everything on from night one."

## Urgency (from sheet)
"First 5,000 families lock in $7.99/month forever. The rest pay $19.99."

## Exclusivity (from sheet)
LP-A: "You are not just joining an app. You are founding the kitchen of the future."
LP-B: "You found this before everyone else. That means something."

## Brand Tone Rules
- Direct, no hype
- Short sentences
- Second person (you/your)
- No exclamation marks
- No Silicon Valley buzzwords
- Honest over sensational
- Say "nine patent-pending technologies" — never "9 patents"
- Say "registered dietitians" — the word "registered" is always required

## Claims Policy
Specific numbers come from ApprovedClaims tab. Use exact_wording from sheet.
Do not invent or round numbers. Do not paraphrase claim wording without approval.
